title: Preschool Referral Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Preschool Referral Programs 20 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF DEPARTMENT OF EARLY LEARNING AND CARE DEPARTMENT OF EARLY LEARNING AND CARE CHILD CARE PRESCHOOL PROMISE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps locate publicly funded preschools in family care, center, and school settings. Eligible students are matched to sites based on family preference and need. Setting options include days, hours of operation, transportation, primary language, bilingual programs, curriculum, and approach. Website Eligibility: -Must be ages 3-4 before September 1st of the calendar year -Household income must be below 200% of the federal poverty level -Must be an Oregon resident -Families receiving SNAP do not need to provide additional income verification -Housing Adjustment: Eligibility Specialists may adjust a family’s gross income calculation when determining eligibility to account for excessive housing costs. This applies to families who do not meet income eligibility but spend more than 30% of their gross income on housing costs. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT PRESCHOOL PROMISE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps locate publicly funded preschools in family care, center, and school settings. In order to lower the incidences of child abuse and neglect, increase effective parenting, and improve family health and well-being via parent education trainings, strengthening linkages among providers, and improving access to support for all families. Coordinated enrollment into subsidized preschool, including Head Start and Preschool Promise. Serves as the Coordinate Early Learning Hub for the county. 821 Main Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-8 Families at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for subsidized preschool enrollment Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (971) 288-8264 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING HUB OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY PRESCHOOL PROMISE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps locate publicly funded preschools in family care, center, and school settings. Website Eligibility: Children must be 3 or 4 years old on or before September 1 of the preschool year, and have a parent or legal guardian living in Multnomah County There are no income eligibility requirements. Children who currently have the least access to high-quality early learning experiences are prioritized in the outreach and application processes.
